info.name: REST API
info.description:
This template contains a boilerplate for documentation that you can quickly customize and reuse.

### How to use this template:

- Replace the content given brackets (()) with your API's details.
    
- Tips are formatted in `codespan` - feel free to read and remove them.
    

---

`Start with a brief overview of what your API offers.`

The ((product name)) provides many API products, tools, and resources that enable you to ((add product value here)).

`You can also list the APIs you offer, link to the relevant pages, or do both in this section.`

## **Getting started guide**

`List the steps or points required to start using your APIs. Make sure to cover everything required to reach success with your API as quickly as possible.`

To start using the ((add APIs here)), you need to -

`The points given below are from The Postman API's documentation. You can reference it to write your own getting started guide.`

- You must use a valid API Key to send requests to the API endpoints. You can get your API key from Postman's [integrations dashboard](https://go.postman.co/settings/me/api-keys).
    
- The API has [rate and usage limits](https://postman.postman.co/workspace/Collection-Templates~6311738d-2e70-441f-ae12-78caf078c5b7/collection/22517504-e9c28f47-1253-44af-a2f3-20dce4da1f18?ctx=documentation#rate-and-usage-limits).
    
- The API only responds to HTTPS-secured communications. Any requests sent via HTTP return an HTTP 301 redirect to the corresponding HTTPS resources.
    
- The API returns request responses in JSON format. When an API request returns an error, it is sent in the JSON response as an error key.
    

## Authentication

`Add details on the authorization keys/tokens required, steps that cover how to get them, and the relevant error codes.`

The ((product name)) API uses ((add your API's authorization type)) for authentication.

`The details given below are from the Postman API's documentation. You can reference it to write your own authentication section.`

Postman uses API keys for authentication. You can generate a Postman API key in the [API keys](https://postman.postman.co/settings/me/api-keys) section of your Postman account settings.

You must include an API key in each request to the Postman API with the X-Api-Key request header.

### Authentication error response

If an API key is missing, malformed, or invalid, you will receive an HTTP 401 Unauthorized response code.

## Rate and usage limits

`Use this section to cover your APIs' terms of use. Include API limits, constraints, and relevant error codes, so consumers understand the permitted API usage and practices.`

`The example given below is from The Postman API's documentation. Use it as a reference to write your APIs' terms of use.`

API access rate limits apply at a per-API key basis in unit time. The limit is 300 requests per minute. Also, depending on your plan, you may have usage limits. If you exceed either limit, your request will return an HTTP 429 Too Many Requests status code.

Each API response returns the following set of headers to help you identify your use status:

| Header | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `X-RateLimit-Limit` | The maximum number of requests that the consumer is permitted to make per minute. |
| `X-RateLimit-Remaining` | The number of requests remaining in the current rate limit window. |
| `X-RateLimit-Reset` | The time at which the current rate limit window resets in UTC epoch seconds. |

### 503 response

An HTTP `503` response from our servers indicates there is an unexpected spike in API access traffic. The server is usually operational within the next five minutes. If the outage persists or you receive any other form of an HTTP `5XX` error, [contact support](https://support.postman.com/hc/en-us/requests/new/).

### **Need some help?**

`Add links that customers can refer to whenever they need help.`

In case you have questions, go through our tutorials ((link to your video or help documentation here)). Or visit our FAQ page ((link to the relevant page)).

Or you can check out our community forum, there’s a good chance our community has an answer for you. Visit our developer forum ((link to developer forum)) to review topics, ask questions, and learn from others.

`You can also document or add links to libraries, code examples, and other resources needed to make a request.`

This endpoint proxies RFC 7591 Dynamic Client Registration requests
to OAuth authorization servers that support DCR.

Proxy OAuth token exchange request to avoid CORS issues.

Uses alita_sdk's exchange_oauth_token function to perform the token exchange.
If toolkit_id is provided, credentials are fetched from the database.

Sync/fetch tools from a remote MCP server.

This endpoint discovers available tools from a remote MCP server.
If the server requires OAuth authorization, it will emit an
'mcp_authorization_required' socket event with the OAuth metadata.

Args:
    project_id: Project ID
    
Returns:
    Dictionary with tools list or task ID for async operation

Get recommended applications for a user based on usage analytics.

Fallback logic:
1. First try to get most used applications by user in this project from monitoring
2. If no data, get most used applications in this project overall
3. If still no data, return first 5 applications sorted alphabetically

Query parameters:
- limit: Number of applications to return (default: 5, max: 20)

item.name: context_manager

request.description: Get the current context status, token utilization, and analytics for a conversation. Path params: project_id, conversation_id. Requires authentication. Endpoint follows the Pylon plugin structure with /api/v1/ prefix.

Update the context management strategy for a conversation. Requires authentication. Endpoint follows the Pylon plugin structure with /api/v1/ prefix.

Example JSON payload:
{
  "strategy": "window",
  "window_size": 10,
  "token_limit": 4096
}

Update the context management strategy for a conversation. Requires authentication. Endpoint follows the Pylon plugin structure with /api/v1/ prefix.

Example JSON payload:
{
  "strategy": "window",
  "window_size": 10,
  "token_limit": 4096
}

item.description: This folder contains all Context Manager Plugin API endpoints for Pylon plugins, following the /api/v1/ prefix structure. All requests require authentication. Endpoints allow you to manage, analyze, and optimize conversation context, strategies, and summaries. Each request includes a clear description and example payloads or query parameters where applicable. Designed for easy discovery and use by developers integrating with the Context Manager Plugin.

request.description:
### Endpoint Description

This endpoint retrieves execution traces for a specified project within the monitoring hub. It allows users to filter and customize the trace data based on various parameters.

### Request Parameters

The following query parameters can be used in the request:

- **entity_id** (integer, required):
    
    - The ID of the agent or entity whose traces you want to retrieve.
        
- **entity_version** (integer, optional):
    
    - The specific version of the entity to trace. If not provided, the latest version will be used.
        
- **days** (integer, optional):
    
    - The time window in days for filtering traces. The default value is 30 days if not specified.
        
- **include_nested** (boolean, optional):
    
    - A flag to include nested agent calls in the traces. Defaults to `true` if not specified.
        
- **target_project_id** (integer, optional, admin only):
    
    - The target project ID for cross-project access. This parameter is restricted to admin users.
        

### Example Request

```
GET {{full_path}}/monitoring_hub/execution_traces/{{project_id}}?entity_id={entity_id}&entity_version={entity_version}&days={days}&include_nested={include_nested}&target_project_id={target_project_id}

 ```

### Response

The response will include execution trace data based on the specified parameters, providing insights into the performance and behavior of the specified entity within the defined time window.

request.description:
Test a specific tool within a toolkit, including its configurations and settings.

**Method**: POST

**URL**:  
`{{full_path}}/applications/test_tool/prompt_lib/{{project_id}}/{{toolkit_id}}?await_response=true`

**Description**:  
This API tests a specific tool within a toolkit and validates its configuration. It supports both synchronous and asynchronous execution modes.

---

#### Query Parameters

- **`await_response`** (optional):  
  - **Type**: Boolean  
  - **Default**: `true`  
  - **Description**:  
    - `true`: Waits for the task to complete and returns the full response.  
    - `false`: Returns a task ID for streaming the response via WebSocket using `sid`.

---

#### Request Body

```json
{
    "user_input": "go",
    "output": [],
    "input_variables": [],
    "testing_type": "tool",
    "llm_settings": {
        "temperature": 0.7,
        "max_tokens": 1512,
        "top_p": 0.8,
        "top_k": 20,
        "model_name": "{{ai_model_name}}",
        "integration_uid": "{{integration_uid}}"
    },
    "tool": "list_files_in_main_branch",
    "sid": null
}
```

- **Fields**:
  - **`user_input`**:  
    - **Type**: String  
    - **Description**: Input text for testing the tool.
  - **`output`**:  
    - **Type**: Array  
    - **Description**: Output data from the tool (if any).
  - **`input_variables`**:  
    - **Type**: Array  
    - **Description**: Variables required for the tool's execution.
  - **`testing_type`**:  
    - **Type**: String  
    - **Default**: `tool`  
    - **Description**: Specifies the type of testing (e.g., `tool` or other types).
  - **`llm_settings`**:  
    - **Type**: Object  
    - **Description**: Configuration settings for the language model.  
    - **Fields**:
      - **`temperature`**: Controls randomness in the output.
      - **`max_tokens`**: Maximum number of tokens in the response.
      - **`top_p`**: Probability threshold for nucleus sampling.
      - **`top_k`**: Limits the sampling pool to the top `k` tokens.
      - **`model_name`**: Name of the AI model to use.
      - **`integration_uid`**: Unique identifier for the integration.
  - **`tool`**:  
    - **Type**: String  
    - **Description**: Name of the tool to test.
  - **`sid`**:  
    - **Type**: String or null  
    - **Description**: Session ID for streaming responses (if applicable).

---

#### Responses

- **`200 OK`**:  
  - Returns the result of the tool test, including any output or errors.
- **`400 Bad Request`**:  
  - Returns an error message if the request is invalid or the tool configuration is incorrect.
- **`500 Internal Server Error`**:  
  - Returns an error message if an unexpected issue occurs during execution.

---

#### Notes

- The `toolkit_config` field now includes expanded settings and fallback mechanisms for missing fields.
- Enhanced validation ensures that all required fields are present and correctly formatted.
- Improved logging provides better insights into the execution process.

request.description:
Imports application data into the project. **Deprecated** in favor of magic import functionality.

**Method**: POST

**Path Parameters**:
- `project_id` (int): The ID of the project.

**Request Body**:
- A JSON array containing application data to import.

**Responses**:
- `201 Created`: Returns the list of successfully created applications and any errors encountered.

**Notes**:
- Requires permissions: `models.applications.export_import.import`.
- Recommended roles: Admin, Editor.
- Viewers are not allowed to use this API.

Publishes a specific version of an application.

**Method**: POST

**Path Parameters**:
- `project_id` (integer): The ID of the project.
- `version_id` (integer): The ID of the application version to publish.

**Responses**:
- `200 OK`: Returns a success message when the application version is published.
- `400 Bad Request`: Returns an error message if the version is not found, is the "latest" version, or if any other issue occurs.
Exports toolkit data for specified IDs. Allows for exporting data as a file or in JSON format.
Updates an existing application with new details, such as version information.
Deletes a specific application from the project using its ID.
Stops an ongoing prediction task for a specific application.
Unpublishes a specific application version by changing its status to 'draft.'

request.description:
### Predict Application

Initiates a prediction task for a specific application version.

**Method**: POST

**URL**: `{{full_path}}/applications/predict/prompt_lib/{{project_id}}/{{agent_version_id}}`

**Request Body**:

- `chat_history`: An array of previous chat messages (default: empty array).
    
- `user_input`: The current user input for which prediction is required.
    

**Responses**:

- `200 OK`: Returns the prediction result.
    
- `400 Bad Request`: Returns an error message if the request is malformed.
    
- `404 Not Found`: Returns an error if the application version is not found.
